# AgentAuth

> OAuth-for-agents: a dead-simple, MCP-native OAuth token broker that lets an AI agent securely
> act on a user's behalf across third-party APIs (Gmail, Slack, GitHub, Notion). The user's OAuth
> token is vaulted server-side (envelope-encrypted, AES-256-GCM) and never exposed to the agent or LLM.

Core idea: your agent makes a call by `(provider, end_user_id)` plus a broker secret key. The broker
injects the user's token server-side and returns the provider's response. No webhooks, no token
handling in your app, no refresh logic. If the user isn't connected yet, you get an
`authorization_required` result carrying a connect URL to send them to.

## REST API

Base URL: your broker (e.g. http://localhost:8787). Auth: `Authorization: Bearer sk_...` (secret key).

- `GET /v1/providers` — list supported providers
- `PUT /v1/integrations/{provider}` — configure a provider OAuth client: `{ client_id, client_secret, scopes }`
- `POST /v1/connect_sessions` — `{ end_user_id, provider, scopes? }` → `{ url }`; send the user to `url` to authorize
- `GET /v1/connect/callback` — OAuth redirect target (public; vaults the token)
- `POST /v1/connections/{provider}/{end_user_id}/token` — fresh access token (auto-refreshed); `?force_refresh=true` to force
- `ALL /v1/proxy/{provider}/{end_user_id}/{path}` — call a provider API as the user (token injected server-side)
- `GET /v1/connections` — list connections
- `DELETE /v1/connections/{provider}/{end_user_id}` — revoke

## TypeScript SDK (npm package: `agentauth`)

```ts
import { AgentAuth, AuthorizationRequiredError } from 'agentauth';
const aa = new AgentAuth({ apiKey: process.env.AGENTAUTH_API_KEY! });

// Act as the user — token injected server-side, never exposed:
const profile = await aa.proxy({ userId, provider: 'github', path: '/user' });

// proxy() and getToken() throw AuthorizationRequiredError (with .connectUrl) when not connected.
await aa.connect({ userId, provider });        // -> { url } to send the user to
await aa.getToken({ userId, provider });       // -> { accessToken, expiresAt }
```

## MCP server

Exposes the broker to AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor) as tools:
`list_providers`, `check_connection`, `create_connect_link`, `call_provider`.
`call_provider` returns an `authorization_required` result with a connect link when the user
isn't connected — the agent surfaces it to the user, then retries.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): setup, architecture, MCP config
- [SDK reference](sdk/README.md)
